notes: Declarative typed relations. Every entry links a source feature id to related-feature ids across layers.

related.explanation: The Thar physiographic region hosts India's hot-desert BWh climate.
Sandy Aridisols are the parent material across the Thar.
Prosopis-Ziziphus-Capparis thorn scrub dominates the region.
Sewan grass patches persist between dunes.
The Tal Chhapar grasslands are the Thar's classic protected grassland.
Alfisols weather from the Aravalli's crystalline base.
Shallow rocky Entisols cap the ridges themselves.
Mount Abu's semi-evergreen pocket is the range's montane exemplar.
Inceptisols and Entisols line the Banas–Chambal valleys.
Vertisols (medium-black cotton soils) blanket the Hadoti plateau.
The basin's trunk river.
The park sits at the Banganga–Gambhir confluence.
The basin's headwaters rise in the Sariska hills.
Endorheic drainage concentrates salts in the Sambhar basin.
DNP's sewan grasslands are the Great Indian Bustard's last stronghold.
Mount Abu is the wettest pocket of Rajasthan.
Canal-command alluvial soils created by IGNP delivery.
Sub-soil salinity is chronic across the internal-drainage zone.
India's largest inland salt lake — the source of the saline soil type here.
